texts.title: Ja nus hons pris — opening stanza
texts.source: Richard I, prison song; Chansonnier tradition (e.g. BnF fr. 1598, Chansonnier d'Arras)
texts.transliteration: Ja nus hons pris ne dira sa raison
texts.translation: No captive man can tell his tale truly
texts.line_ipa: [ʒa nys õs pris nə diˈra sa raˈzũ]
texts.pronunciation_model: reconstructed Anglo-Norman / Old French (12th-c. Oïl register)
texts.notes: Attested lyric attributed to Richard I in manuscript tradition; not a chronicle quotation of spoken dialogue.

texts.title: Crusade acclamation — Deus le volt
texts.source: Crusade chronicle corpus (Itinerarium Regis Ricardi; Roger of Hoveden tradition)
texts.notes: Attested crusade formula in Latin and vernacular chronicle transmission; pronunciation reconstructed.

texts.title: Marie de France, Lais — authorial prologue
texts.source: Marie de France, Lais, General Prologue
texts.transliteration: Li nom est Marie, e si sui de France
texts.translation: My name is Marie, and I am from France
texts.line_ipa: [li nɔm ɛst maˈri e si sy də ˈfrãːsə]
texts.notes: Attested Anglo-Norman authorial statement; period court literary corpus — not Eleanor of Aquitaine's direct speech.

texts.title: Eleanor of Aquitaine — surviving voice register
texts.source: Eleanor's surviving documents (charters, letters) — predominantly Latin
texts.translation: Eleanor's attested words survive chiefly in Latin charters and chronicle paraphrase, not Anglo-Norman direct speech.
texts.notes: Corpus honesty marker: do not invent Anglo-Norman quotations attributed to Eleanor.
